commit.message:
fix(playwright): early_exit code-0 success path, DEMO_RECORD_VIDEO blocklist, pause_at_end auto-disable (#33)

Three bug fixes in run_demo() and check_demo_result():

- early_exit code 0: when the demo process exits naturally within the
  monitoring window (code 0), treat it as successful completion instead
  of a crash. Creates a proper DemoRunState entry with artifact scanning,
  trace parsing, and video persistence so check_demo_result lookups work.

- DEMO_RECORD_VIDEO blocklist: added DEMO_RECORD_VIDEO to
  EXTRA_ENV_BLOCKED_PREFIXES in helpers.ts so extra_env cannot silently
  override the explicit record_video parameter.

- pause_at_end auto-disable: compute effectivePauseAtEnd that forces
  pause_at_end=false when record_video=true, since page.pause() prevents
  context.close() which is required to finalize the .webm video file.

Updates docs/CLAUDE-REFERENCE.md to document all three behaviors.
Adds 289 new behavior tests in server-behaviors.test.ts and 114 in
playwright.test.ts covering the new code paths.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hint pipeline with HintSource trait + --intent CLI flag

Hint pipeline (trait-based, pluggable):
- HintSource trait: Send + Sync, implement to add custom hints
- Built-in sources: PatternHints, ToolHints, WorkflowHints
- collect_hints() runs intent guard + all sources, deduplicates
- default_sources() returns all built-ins
- No tracking state — just stateless advice per step

Built-in hints:
- Pattern: "read before writing" on step 1
- Tool: suggest codegraph MCP on early search steps
- Workflow: TDD reminder on writes, git commit reminder on late executes
- Intent: Ask/Plan mode restrictions (from intent_guard)

CLI: --intent auto|ask|build|plan for headless mode

commit.message:
Fix auth redirect loop: make Convex session creation non-fatal in landing callback

If the Convex mutation failed (wrong URL, schema error, network issue) it threw
into the outer catch handler, redirecting to /auth/login?error=... which sent
the user back to WorkOS → callback → fail → WorkOS → ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS.

Wrap the Convex mutation in its own try/catch so session creation failure is
logged but does not abort the callback. Cookies are still set so the
cookie-based session fallback in /api/auth/session handles auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: email preview/send newline mismatch and Sentry proxy body error

Pass { breaks: true, gfm: true } explicitly to all marked() call sites
so email preview and server-side send produce identical HTML. Previously
the preview relied on leaked global marked config while the server used
defaults, causing newlines to be silently dropped in sent emails.

Also exclude /ingest and /tunnel proxy routes from Sentry's server-side
HTTP instrumentation to fix "Response body object should not be disturbed
or locked" errors.

commit.message:
Embed migrations in Nitro server plugin

Moves auto-migration from the Docker entrypoint into a Nitro server
plugin (server/plugins/migrate.ts) that runs on every app startup in
production. This is resilient to docker restart, image upgrades, and
any container management that bypasses the entrypoint.

The plugin:
- Only runs when NODE_ENV=production (dev/test use pnpm db:migrate)
- Baselines databases previously set up via db:push (idempotent)
- Calls drizzle migrate() to apply any unapplied migrations
- Exits the process if migration fails so the container doesn't start
  with a broken schema

The entrypoint script is simplified to just start the server since the
plugin handles migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix L-01/L-02/L-05: Chunked campaign sending with resume, atomic cron claims, no PII in response

Rewrite campaign-sender to process BATCH_SIZE=5 contacts per cron tick with
immediate send log writes (survives timeouts). Resume support via SendLog
lookup skips already-processed contacts. Cron route processes one campaign
per invocation, resumes "sending" first, claims "scheduled" atomically via
updateMany. Response returns counts only — no recipient emails leaked to
cron-job.org. Updated tests (10/10 passing) including new resume test.

Fix attestation submit: add hasColumn guards for schema-safe validation

Production submit route was unconditionally checking for structured columns
(revenue_driver, foh_staffing_decision, etc.) that don't exist in the DB yet.
Now falls back to legacy fields (revenue_notes, labor_notes) when structured
columns are absent. Also adds auth guards (requireUser, assertVenueAccess)
to all attestation API routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ml): CW-121 wide stance training — speed PASS, stability FAIL

1M steps from-scratch training with wider hip stance (±0.14m).
Stability-first rewards (orientation=-15, speed=7).

Results (10ep x 1000 steps):
  Speed: 0.369 m/s — PASS (target ≥0.3)
  Avg tilt: 6.4° — FAIL (target <5°)
  Max tilt: 15.6° — hits termination boundary immediately
  Survival: 1.2s avg — FAIL (target ≥15s)

Wide stance improves speed (0.37 vs CW-115's 0.28 m/s) but does NOT
solve stability from scratch in 1M steps. Policy learned "go fast
before falling" instead of stable locomotion. May need:
- More training steps (3-5M)
- Fine-tuning from a stable checkpoint instead of from-scratch
- Different reward balance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add background cron endpoint for room sync without open clients

New /api/cron/sync-rooms endpoint that syncs all active rooms:
- Checks Spotify playback state for each room
- Auto-advances to next song when current finishes
- Pre-queues next song in a wider 45s window (was 5s) for gapless
  playback even with 10-30s cron intervals
- Protected by CRON_SECRET to prevent abuse
- Handles token refresh, debounce, and all edge cases

Rooms now keep playing correctly even when the host closes their
browser, as long as the cron service is pinging the endpoint.

commit.message:
Fix: pin Python 3.12 for Streamlit Cloud compatibility

Streamlit Cloud updated its default runtime to Python 3.13.
numpy~=1.26.0, pandas~=2.1.0 and scikit-learn~=1.4.0 have no
pre-built wheels for Python 3.13 and fail to compile from source
(_PyLong_AsByteArray signature change breaks Cython extensions).
Adding .python-version=3.12 pins the runtime to Python 3.12 which
provides pre-built wheels for all pinned dependencies.

Also removed .python-version from .gitignore so the file is tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Limit same attacker composition puzzles and boost diversity weights

nonce with unsafe-inline to match next.config.ts fallback.

commit.message:
feat: columnar Shard v2 reader for selective index loading

Split index exports into separate Shard entries (config, vectors,
deleted) instead of one monolithic blob. ColumnReader API lets callers
read metadata/config without loading vectors — useful for collection
inspection, migration planning, and partial rebuilds.

Implements ColumnExportable on HNSW, FLAT, and IVF indexes. Binary
vector packing (uint64 ID + float32[] row) with zstd achieves ~37%
compression. Backward-compatible: ColumnReader falls back gracefully
on monolithic v1 shards.

commit.message:
fix: Codex CLI auth, CWD, and context injection for K3s pod

Three issues prevented Codex CLI from working in the nexus pod:

1. Auth: Rust-based Codex CLI ignores OPENAI_API_KEY env var — needs
   explicit `codex login --with-api-key`. Added setup-codex init
   container that pipes the key from nexus-env secret at pod startup.

2. CWD: CODEX_CWD defaulted to /home/puretensorai (host path) which
   doesn't exist in the container. Changed to /app.

3. Context injection: Codex CLI ignored <system> blocks in user prompts.
   Switched to writing AGENTS.md in the CWD before each call — Codex
   auto-reads this file as developer instructions, ensuring system
   prompt and memory context reach the model properly.

Also improved error handling: catch RuntimeError from empty streams and
surface auth failures as user-friendly messages instead of raw tracebacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
